dismissalText.commentary: Sai Sudharsan b Mukesh Kumar 12 (7b 2x4 0x6 12m) SR: 171.42

dismissalComment.html: Chopped on, Mukesh points straight and leaps. 133ks seam-up length ball moves in off the pitch, causes Sudharsan to get an under-edge as he looks for a cross-bat square drive. Slices back onto the stumps, the bent elbows show how he's cramped for room. Orthodox batter done in by orthodox seam bowling

dismissalText.commentary: Shubman Gill c Rana b Ngidi 70 (45b 4x4 5x6 93m) SR: 155.55

dismissalComment.html: taken at short mid-off!! Another one at a lack of pace, and Gill is gone. He went lofting at this full delivery outside off, but seemed to be beaten by the slower ball. Gill opened the face of the bat to try and slice it - perhaps - but ended up skying the ball to where a short mid-off would've been. Rana did well to keep his eyes on it

dismissalText.commentary: Jos Buttler b Kuldeep Yadav 52 (27b 3x4 5x6 27m) SR: 192.59

dismissalComment.html: Knocked over, Kuldeep gets Buttler off his third ball. Was a long-hop, but at 95ks after bowling 89ks last two balls, and it didn't bounce. Kept low, lost a lot of pace off the pitch and beat the pull as Buttler got low and tried to adjust. Bottom hand came off as the bails flew

It was a nice grab as he kept his eyes on the ball and timed his dive perfectly

dismissalText.commentary: Kuldeep Yadav run out (†Buttler) 1 (1b 0x4 0x6 6m) SR: 100

dismissalComment.html: Have GT have won by one run?! This is an amazing finish. The umpire has given Kuldeep run-out, but hang on... this could be wide for height. Plenty is happening even as GT look confident. The players are lurking on the field, waiting for the final decision. Ohh, the ball passes below Miller's head, although he's "on his toes", as per the third umpire. More wait. More nerves. More drama. If it's not a wide, GT will win by one run. Indeed. The ball passes well below the head height. So it's not a wide. So it's confirmed: GT win by one run. This was the delivery: Prasidh bowled a slower one, and short in length. Miller looked to pull but missed. The ball shaped away just before landing into Buttler's gloves. Miller and Kuldeep ran, but the latter fell short despite diving as Buttler hit the stumps at his end

dismissalText.commentary: Axar Patel c Phillips b Rashid Khan 2 (3b 0x4 0x6 3m) SR: 66.66

dismissalComment.html: Phillips does what he does best - takes a good catch!! Rashid went across the stumps, and bowled from around the wicket. It pitched on a full length on off, and Rashid bowled the wrong 'un. Axar went lofting by opening the face of his bat, but ended up slicing it for Phillips, who, at extra cover, turned around and dived, with the ball dropping over his head. It was a nice grab as he kept his eyes on the ball and timed his dive perfectly

dismissalComment.html: direct hit, and Stubbs is gone!! Sai was at mid-on, and had a lot of time to aim at the bowler's end as Stubbs had taken off. But Stubbs was sent back after running halfway down, and even a dive couldn't save him. Siraj bowled another bouncer at Rahul, who swatted at it from around off stump. The ball only went as far as mid-on, though, and Stubbs ran without looking back

dismissalText.commentary: KL Rahul c †Buttler b Mohammed Siraj 92 (52b 11x4 4x6 99m) SR: 176.92

dismissalComment.html: got him!! Rahul falls for 92. This could be the game for GT. Rahul chases a wide delivery, and falls. A cross-seam delivery from Siraj, who bowls on a yorker length and wide outside off. Rahul had left the previous wide balls outside off this over, but not this time. He has a swing at it, and ends up getting an outside edge for Buttler

dismissalText.commentary: Kuldeep Yadav run out (†Buttler) 1 (1b 0x4 0x6 6m) SR: 100

dismissalComment.html: Have GT have won by one run?! This is an amazing finish. The umpire has given Kuldeep run-out, but hang on... this could be wide for height. Plenty is happening even as GT look confident. The players are lurking on the field, waiting for the final decision. Ohh, the ball passes below Miller's head, although he's "on his toes", as per the third umpire. More wait. More nerves. More drama. If it's not a wide, GT will win by one run. Indeed. The ball passes well below the head height. So it's not a wide. So it's confirmed: GT win by one run. This was the delivery: Prasidh bowled a slower one, and short in length. Miller looked to pull but missed. The ball shaped away just before landing into Buttler's gloves. Miller and Kuldeep ran, but the latter fell short despite diving as Buttler hit the stumps at his end
